criteria.name: `TOPIC_EDITOR_TOOLS` exposes exactly one `search_messages` read tool using the unified filter schema and includes a separate `get_reply_chain` tool with required `message_id` and optional `max_depth`.

The old topic-editor `search_messages` parameters `author_id`, `channel_id`, and `hours_back` are no longer part of that tool schema, and equivalent behavior is available through `from_author_id`, `in_channel_id`, `after`, and default `scope='window'`.

Window-scope `search_messages` AND-combines query, from_author, in_channel, mentions_author (via `<@ID>` / `<@!ID>` regex in content), has, time, reply-status, and limit filters against `dispatcher_context['messages']`.

Archive-scope `search_messages` calls a storage-backed `search_messages_unified` path scoped by `guild_id` and `environment`, with parameterized content matching, mention-token `.ilike()` patterns, and compact result rows with `content_preview` ≤200 chars.
Malformed `after` or `before` values (not ISO timestamp, not relative `Nd`/`Nh`) produce a `tool_error` outcome rather than being silently ignored.
`get_reply_chain` walks `discord_messages.reference_id` iteratively, clamps `max_depth` to 1–15 (default 5), detects cycles via a `seen` set, and returns compact ancestor rows root-first.

The topic-editor system prompt documents the unified search examples and `get_reply_chain` usage without modifying `src/features/summarising/live_update_editor.py` or `src/features/summarising/live_update_prompts.py`.

Focused topic-editor tests pass after updating existing `search_messages` tests (param rename: `author_id`→`from_author_id`, `channel_id`→`in_channel_id`) and adding coverage for archive dispatch, filter combinations, reply-chain walking, and cycle detection.

No guarded flows are changed: `finalize_run`, the agent loop, override-collision, idempotency, lease-expiry, force-close, vision tools, and `live_update_editor.py` / `live_update_prompts.py` remain outside the diff except incidental import/schema list updates if required.
Archive `mentions_author_id` filtering uses `.ilike()` on `content` with `<@ID>` and `<@!ID>` token patterns — NO reference to a non-existent `mentions` column.
Implementation keeps search/reply helpers parameterized: no raw SQL string interpolation for user-provided search text; mention-token patterns use integer IDs formatted into fixed strings.
Result payloads remain compact enough for the existing ~2KB tool-result cap; `content_preview` capped at 200 characters per message; list-level `truncated: true` flag when rows are trimmed.

summary: Light robustness: review skipped; stub written for artifact parity.
